This show was done as a performance for Lucas's wedding, where the band performed a set of covers. Cookie played bass instead of Lucas. These Boots Are Made for Walkin' contained a semi-drum solo, where the band played quieter but Eric and Cavs played drums louder and faster for a brief section. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist for this show is believed to be complete. All the songs listed have been confirmed via a series of livestreams on Flightless Record's Instagram, but no setlist exists to confirm if more songs had been played.

Surprise show announced the day before. This show featured the debut of Self-Immolate, Mars For The Rich, Venusian 1, Venusian 2, Perihelion, Inner Cell, Loyalty, Horology, Plastic Boogie, This Thing, The Bird Song, Melting, Boogieman Sam, The Cruel Millenial, Acarine, Murder Of The Universe, Planet B, Organ Farmer, Hell and Cyboogie. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist for this show is believed to be complete, but only partial recordings are available. Mars For The Rich, Planet B and Cyboogie have been confirmed via audio/video recordings. The remaining songs were confirmed through contemporaneous discussions through various social media, but any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (guest appearances, segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net.

Planet B and portions of Self-Immolate and Organ Farmer were released on the PHC FILMS YouTube channel. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist for this show is believed to be complete, but only partial recordings are available. Self-Immolate, Planet B, Gamma Knife through Billabong Valley, Cyboogie through Murder Of The Universe and Organ Farmer have been confirmed via audio/video recordings. The remaining songs were confirmed via the physical written setlist and contemporaneous discussions through various social media, but any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (guest appearances, segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net. Poster By Jason Galea Edition of 100

Venusian 2, Am I In Heaven?, This Thing, Work This Time, Cyboogie, Altered Beast IV, Muddy Water, Melting and Self-Immolate have been confirmed via audio/video recordings. The remaining songs were confirmed via the physical written setlist and contemporaneous discussions through various social media, but any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (guest appearances, segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net. Poster By Jason Galea Edition of 100

This show featured the debut of Real's Not Real. Evil Star was played through the PA speakers before and as the band came on stage. Hot Water was played for the first time since 2017-10-12 (104 show gap) and featured Adam Halliwell (introduced as the band's best friend) on flute. Following Open Water, which featured a drum solo, Joey pretended to be introducing John Henry Bonham playing Moby Dick. Loyalty featured use of a vocoder for Joey's vocals and Stu on flute. After Horology, Joey thanked openers C.O.F.F.I.N, Bitch Diesel, and "our sexy young lad friends Mildlife". Head On/Pill featured Adam Halliwell on flute, Hot Water, Alter Me. Cellophane, I'm In Your Mind, and Am I In Heaven? teases and quotes, and Robot Stop, Cyboogie, Horology, Organ Farmer, and Rattlesnake teases. This show was released through the Official Bootlegger program as Live in Adelaide ‘19. Poster By Jason Galea Edition of 100

Evil Star was played through the PA speakers before and as the band went on stage. Only Part I of The River was performed and it contained Wah Wah teases. Wah Wah contained The River quotes. This Thing was introduced as a song about Joeys mom. Boogieman Sam was dedicated to their sound tech "Sammy José". Cyboogie was introduced as a song about Stu's grandma by Joey, with him also asking the audience for respect to let Stu sing about his grandma. After Cyboogie, Joey thanked openers ORB and Stonefield.

This show took place at the former Fillmore West space. Evil Star was played through the PA speakers before and as the band came on stage. Joey shouted out The Grateful Dead before Mars For the Rich. I'm Not In Your Mind and I'm In Your Mind Fuzz contained Altered Beast teases. Cellophane contained Alter Me quotes and teases. This Thing was introduced by Joey as a "song about communism." Billabong Valley was introduced by Ambrose as a “song about a bush ranger buddy.” Joey thanked ORB and Stonefield before Perihelion.

Evil Star was played through the PA speakers before and as the band went on stage. Work This Time contained a guitar solo for the first time. The Bitter Boogie contained Cyboogie teases and Boogieman Sam quotes. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist is believed to be complete. Planet B through The Fourth Colour, Work This Time, Let Me Mend The Past and Self-Immolate through The Bitter Boogie were confirmed by audio/video recordings. The remaining songs were confirmed via contemporaneous discussions through various social media, but any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (guest appearances, segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net. Poster By Jason Galea Edition of 200

Planet B through The Fourth Colour, Work This Time, Let Me Mend The Past and Self-Immolate through The Bitter Boogie were confirmed by audio/video recordings. The remaining songs were confirmed via contemporaneous discussions through various social media, but any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (guest appearances, segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net. Poster By Jason Galea Edition of 200

showtitle: Infest the Rat's Nest Album Release Show
shownotes:
“Infest the Rats’ Nest” album was released the day of the show. Evil Star was played through the PA speakers before and as the band went on stage. Open Water featured a drum solo. Am I In Heaven? contained Alter Me, Robot Stop and Cellophane teases and quotes and Rattlesnake and Big Fig Wasp teases.

Poster By Jason Galea
Edition of 100

This was an in-studio appearance for radio station KEXP in Seattle. Following the performance the band had an 18 minute interview with KEXP DJ Cheryl Waters.

Evil Star was played through the PA speakers before and as the band went on stage. Work This Time contained a guitar solo. After Doom City, the Han-Tyumi voice said "Thank you Seattle" and a quote from Murder of the Universe multiple times. Wah Wah contained a River intro during which Joey thanked openers ORB and Stonefield, as well as containing The River quotes. 

Poster By Jason Galea
Edition of 100

Evil Star was played through the PA speakers before and as the band went on stage. Mars For The Rich was introduced as a song about communism, followed by an audience member pulling the fire alarm which proceeded to go off for the next few songs. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist is believed to be complete. Cellophane, Inner Cell through People-Vultures and Planet B through The River were confirmed by audio/video recordings. The remaining songs were confirmed via the written setlist and contemporaneous discussions through various social media, but any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (guest appearances, segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net. Poster By Jason Galea Edition of 100

This show was released through the Official Bootlegger program as Live in Milwaukee ‘19 and featured the debut of Down the Sink. Evil Star was played through the PA speakers before the the band came on. Murder Of The Universe was performed without the Han-Tyumi narration due to an error. After this, Ambrose humorously said "Han didn't like you, sorry.". Mars For The Rich was not listed on the physical setlist. Head On/Pill contained Hot Water, Sea Of Trees and Rattlesnake teases, Hot Wax quotes and Alter Me, Am I In Heaven?, Cellophane and I'm In Your Mind teases and quotes as well as featuring Cavs crowdsurfing multiple times throughout the song. Poster By Jason Galea Edition of 100

This show was released through the Official Bootlegger program as Live in Milwaukee ‘19 and featured the debut of Down the Sink. Evil Star was played through the PA speakers before the the band came on. Murder Of The Universe was performed without the Han-Tyumi narration due to an error. After this, Ambrose humorously said "Han didn't like you, sorry.". Mars For The Rich was not listed on the physical setlist. Head On/Pill contained Hot Water, Sea Of Trees and Rattlesnake teases, Hot Wax quotes and Alter Me, Am I In Heaven?, Cellophane and I'm In Your Mind teases and quotes as well as featuring Cavs crowdsurfing multiple times throughout the song. Poster By Jason Galea Edition of 100

Evil Star was played through the PA speakers as the band went on stage. Open Water featured a drum solo. Joey dedicated Work This Time, which contained a guitar solo, to Dan Bilzerian, with him claiming he saw "about 500 Dan Bilzerians" before the show. After Doom City, Ambrose thanked openers ORB and Stonefield. Before Cyboogie, the Han-Tyumi voice was used to say "Hello" repeatedly. Gamma Knife contained Robot Stop teases and quotes. Am I In Heaven? contained Boogieman Sam, Cyboogie and Alter Me quotes and teases and Rattlesnake, Hot Water and Head On/Pill teases.

Evil Star was played through the PA speakers as the band went on stage. Only Part I of The River was performed and it contained Wah Wah teases. Wah Wah contained The River quotes. During The Cruel Millennial and The Wheel, Ambrose wore an audience member's fishie helmet. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist is believed to be complete. Planet B and Plastic Boogie through Self-Immolate were confirmed by audio/video recordings. The remaining songs were confirmed via the written setlist and contemporaneous discussions through various social media, but any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (guest appearances, segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net. Poster By Jason Galea Edition of 100

Planet B and Plastic Boogie through Self-Immolate were confirmed by audio/video recordings. The remaining songs were confirmed via the written setlist and contemporaneous discussions through various social media, but any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (guest appearances, segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net. Poster By Jason Galea Edition of 100

The remaining songs were confirmed via contemporaneous discussions through various social media, but any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (guest appearances, segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete. Sense was on the written setlist after The Bird Song but was replaced with Let Me Mend The Past.

Evil Star was played through the PA speakers as the band came on stage. Sense featured use of a Vocoder during part of the vocals. Before Head On/Pill, the Han-Tyumi voice was used to say "Hello" repeatedly, as well as "Thank you", as the band thanked the crowd and opening acts ORB and Stonefield. Head On/Pill contained Am I In Heaven?, People-Vultures, Alter Me, and Boogieman Sam teases and quotes, and Cyboogie, Robot Stop, I'm In Your Mind, Rattlesnake, and Hot Water teases.

jamchart_notes: Following Cyboogie and Acarine, this version starts pretty normal and they let it ride as they tend to do, but then the vocoder comes back out. Sounds like Cyboogie and Sense had a baby.

Evil Star was played through the PA speakers as the band went on stage. Float Along - Fill Your Lungs was played for the first time known since 2015-12-09. (313 show gap) KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist is believed to be complete. Self-Immolate through The Great Chain of Being, Boogieman Sam and Wah Wah through Float Along - Fill Your Lungs were confirmed by audio/video recordings. The remaining songs were confirmed via contemporaneous discussions through various social media, but any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (guest appearances, segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net. Poster By Jason Galea Edition of 200

The remaining songs were confirmed via contemporaneous discussions through various social media, but any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (guest appearances, segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net. Poster By Jason Galea Edition of 200

This show was released through the Official Bootlegger program as Live in London ‘19. Cellophane contained Alter Me teases and quotes. After Crumbling Castle, which featured the intro for the first known time, Joey commented that the venue was "a bit different from the Shacklewell Arms". Before Float Along- Fill Your Lungs, Joey thanked openers Stonefield and ORB. All of ‘Planet B’ along with portions of ‘Self-Immolate’ and ‘Rattlesnake’ from this show are featured in the ‘Chunky Shrapnel’ album. Poster By Jason Galea Edition of 500

Evil Star played through the PA speakers as the band came on stage. I'm Not In Your Mind contained Robot Stop teases. Wah Wah contained The River intro and teases. Cut Throat Boogie contained The Cruel Millennial outro. Head On/Pill featured Rattlesnake, Mr. Beat and Sea of Trees teases, Robot Stop, Hot Water, and Am I In Heaven? teases and quotes, and Hot Wax quotes. Stu played drums for part of Head On/Pill, with Amby on lead vocals and guitar, and Cavs on keys. Cavs also began diving into the crowd and crowdsurfing during the time when Stu was on drums. Muddy Water was on the setlist, but not played. 'Murder of the Universe' and parts of the 'Head On/Pill' jam from this show are featured in the 'Chunky Shrapnel' concert film.

The River contained Wah Wah teases and quotes. Am I in Heaven? contained Cellophane and Cyboogie teases, and a Head on Pill jam. Venusian 2 through Sense was released through the Official Bootlegger program as part of Live in Brussels ‘19. Cavs’s drum solo during ‘Open Water’ is featured in the ‘Chunky Shrapnel’ album under the name 'Parking'. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist for this show is believed to be complete. All songs besides Doom City have been confirmed via audio/video recordings, and The River is incomplete. Doom City has been confirmed through contemporaneous discussions through various social media. Any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (guest appearances, segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net. Poster By Jason Galea Edition of 400

jamchart_notes: Living up to the song’s name, the band drifts gently into a truly spellbinding jam, moving through some gorgeous guitar melodies which then comes down to staccato intertwining interplay. The guitars then open up into some resplendent and contemplative ambiance before slowly and patiently returning to the song. However it is truly the synths that are the star of the show, holding everything together aloft on a warm glowing cloud, guiding the band in serenity from beginning to end.
shownotes: Work This Time contained a guitar solo. The intro to Robot Stop contained Hot Water and Rattlesnake teases. Alter Me and Altered Beast I were on the setlist after Muddy Water but cut for time. The Wheel through Float Along – Fill Your Lungs was released through the Official Bootlegger program as part of Live in Brussels ‘19. Poster By Jason Galea Edition of 400

This show featured the debut of Dirt. After Doom City, the band said happy birthday to Joey. The Bitter Boogie contained elements of Back Door Man (Howlin' Wolf) as well as Cut Throat Boogie and On The Road Again (Canned Heat) quotes. Gamma Knife featured a Robot Stop tease. During The Bitter Boogie, Ambrose went crowdsurfing. Portions of Sense featured in 'Chunky Shrapnel' film.

Poster By Jason Galea
Edition of 200

jamchart_notes: This energetic version features Ambrose crowd surfing after a soulful "Bitter" call and response. Howlin' Wolf's Back Door Man and Cut Throat Boogie playfulness add to this 11.5 minute boogie.

Doom City was on the written setlist after Billabong Valley but was not played. Wah Wah contained a River intro and The River teases and quotes. Head On/Pill contained a flute solo from Stu, Rattlesnake teases and Alter Me and Am I In Heaven? teases and quotes. Stu’s flute solo from the ‘Head On/Pill’ jam is featured in the ‘Chunky Shrapnel’ album. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist for this show is believed to be complete, but only partial recordings are available. Venusian 2, Hot Water, Wah Wah, Road Train, Boogieman Sam and Head On/Pill have been confirmed via audio/video recordings. The remaining songs were confirmed through contemporaneous discussions through various social media, but any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (guest appearances, segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net. Poster By Jason Galea Edition of 200

KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist for this show is believed to be complete, but only partial recordings are available. Planet B, Horology, Rattlesnake, Open Water, Beginner's Luck and The River have been confirmed via audio/video recordings. The remaining songs were confirmed through contemporaneous discussions through various social media, but any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (guest appearances, segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net. Poster By Jason Galea Edition of 200

Evil Star was played through the PA speakers before and as the band came on stage. After Billabong Valley a kid was invited on stage to hug the band. Am I In Heaven? contained teases and quotes of Pill, Boogieman Sam, and Alter Me. Organ Farmer was on the setlist before Am I in Heaven? but wasn't played. This show was released through the Official Bootlegger program as Live in Paris ‘19. Poster by Jason Galea Edition of 200

Evil Star was played through the PA speakers before and as the band came on stage. The Bitter Boogie contained Cut Throat Boogie and On The Road Again (Canned Heat) quotes. Han-Tyumi, The Confused Cyborg was played for the first time since 2018-03-16. (107 show gap). ‘Venusian 2’, ‘Hell’, and portions of ‘The Bitter Boogie’ from this show are featured in the ‘Chunky Shrapnel’ album. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist is confirmed to be complete based on the attached multicam compilation. However, all the videos are incomplete and any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (guest appearances, segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete. If anyone has any additional information about this show or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net. Poster By Jason Galea Edition of 70

Evil Star was played through the PA speakers as the band went on stage. Cut Throat Boogie contained a Cruel Millennial outro. Wah Wah contained a River intro as well as The River teases and quotes. During the River intro of Wah Wah an audience member got on stage, who Joey then passed his microphone to before security escorted them offstage. Am I In Heaven? contained Robot Stop, Head On/Pill, Cellophane and I'm In Your Mind quotes and teases, Boogieman Sam and Hot Wax quotes and Hot Water teases, as well as featuring Stu on vocoder and later flute for an extended Hot Water jam which was followed by Cavs crowd surfing while Stu played drums. Let Me Mend the Past and Wah Wah from this show are featured in the Chunky Shrapnel album.

Poster By Jason Galea
Edition of 100

jamchart_notes: After an energetic intro to the song, the band launch into an extended "medley" section followed Stu briefly trying out his vocoder. He quickly swaps to the flute, which the band follows by switching keys and going into a 3 minute long Hot Water jam. After returning back to Heaven?, the band begin jamming before bringing the tempo down for Cavs to crowdsurf while Stu takes over on drums.
